provenance.composition_title: Prelude in E Minor, Op. 28 No. 4
provenance.verdict_reason: Prelude in E Minor, Op. 28 No. 4 (1839) by Frederic Chopin. Composition: US=public_domain, EU=public_domain. Arrangement by Bernd Krueger. License: CC-BY-SA. Evidence: https://piano-midi.de/. All initial public_candidate rules met. Awaiting Slice 2.5 verification: source URL resolves, license text preserved at source, license version confirmed. Until verified, treat as internal for distribution.

annotation_target.structure: Climactic approach — RH ascends, bass deepens, maximum tension
key_moments: m15 harmonic peak — most distant from tonic
teaching_goals: shape the climax dynamically — this is the piece's emotional center
RH ascent should feel like a desperate reach
style_tips: allow a crescendo to mf here — the piece earns it
don't rush — the climax must breathe

teaching_notes.note: The only moment in the piece to lean into — controlled emotion, not hysteria.

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 13–16 of Prelude in E Minor, Op. 28 No. 4 (continuation of mm. 9–12), analyze the musical continuation, play in a loop.

session.content: Play measures 13–16 and describe the intensification.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 13–16.

Measures 13–16 push toward the climax with more harmonic intensity. The bass is now far from the opening E, and the RH melody reaches upward in response. This is the prelude's expressive peak approach. Let me loop mm. 13–16.
Chopin Prelude E minor mm. 13–16: intensification toward climax, RH reaches upward, harmonic peak. Pairs with mm. 9–12 descent.
